As a proud graduate of the Virtual University of Pakistan, I have a largely positive view of my experience there. The flexibility of online learning was one of the aspects I appreciated the most. It allowed me to balance my studies with other commitments, which was essential for my personal and professional growth. What I Liked: Flexible Learning Environment: The online format enabled me to study at my own pace. This was particularly beneficial during my busy semesters, as I could manage my time effectively. Resource Availability: The university offered a wealth of online resources, including recorded lectures, e-books, and discussion forums that fostered a supportive learning environment. Diverse Curriculum: The Business Administration program had a well-rounded curriculum that covered essential topics, equipping me with practical knowledge and skills that are relevant in the workforce. Supportive Faculty: The faculty members were approachable and responsive. They were always ready to help with any inquiries, which enhanced my learning experience. Yes, I would recommend the Virtual University of Pakistan to students who are looking for flexibility and a quality education in Business Administration. It’s an excellent choice for self-motivated individuals who want to advance their careers while managing other responsibilities. However, potential students should be aware of the challenges of online learning, especially in terms of networking.

The BSCS program at Virtual University has provided me with a solid foundation in computer science. The flexibility of online learning is perfect for managing studies alongside other responsibilities. I didn’t come in with unrealistic expectations or anything but what I experienced was still shocking. Don’t believe their fake AI generated replies they leave on reviews on google or anywhere. I’ve dealt with their support myself as a former student and it’s obvious no one there understands proper English. Every response feels like copy paste nonsense written just to shut you up. The study material is laughably outdated. I’m talking early 2000s outdated. As someone who has a business and does a lot of freelancing, I see what modern teams and a lot of companies actually use, this curriculum is embarrassing. While the rest of the world teaches python, modern tools, real world business applications, VU is stuck and teaches you obsolete concepts that are super irrelevant today. When I showed this material to people I work with internationally, they literally laughed. Not at me but at how backward and pathetic the system still is in Pakistan. The handouts are awful and its painfully obvious they weren’t written for Pakistani students at all. You can literally tell from the wording, examples and tone that the target audience is European and North American (US Canada etc). Its just copied material from foreign textbooks, badly edited and very poorly adapted. When they try to change things to make it sound “Pakistani”, the grammar falls apart lol, which makes it even more embarrassing. Clearly no one competent wrote or reviewed this. The LMS is just as bad. In 2026, lectures are still dumped on YouTube like some random coaching center. The website crashes randomly and I’ve personally seen error messages in Chinese, which tells you how cheap and outsourced the whole thing is. Its as bad as any government website in lumber 1 Pakistan. Especially if you’re waiting for results coz good luck. The moment traffic increases, their systems panic and crash. Assignment and GDB checking are also super bad. Grades feel random and sometimes mood-based, feedback from their “qualified professors” also never makes sense. They also ask students to upload screenshots as “proof” of completed work at times because the university doesn’t even have basic systems to track assignments. Imagine paying tuition to an institution that runs on screenshots lol. Honestly if you just want a piece of paper instead of actually learning something, go for it. This place is made exactly for people like you. Outdated material, incompetent staff, broken systems, zero accountability. Based on my own experience I wouldn’t recommend Virtual University of Pakistan to anyone who values their time, money or future.

The University of Peshawar, nestled in the heart of the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province of Pakistan, stands as a venerable institution that has been shaping minds and fostering academic excellence for decades. Having had the privilege of experiencing this institution firsthand, I can confidently say that it is a beacon of higher education in the region. The University of Peshawar boasts a diverse range of academic programs, covering a spectrum of disciplines. Its faculties of science, arts, humanities, social sciences, and technology are well-established and offer a rich academic experience. The quality of education and research, while generally commendable, can vary between departments. The university’s faculty is a mixed bag. While there are highly knowledgeable and dedicated professors who inspire and mentor students, there are also instances of subpar teaching and lack of engagement. The campus is sprawling and picturesque, providing a conducive environment for learning and extracurricular activities. The library resources are decent, although further investment is needed to keep up with modern research needs. The University of Peshawar offers a vibrant and culturally rich campus life. Various student societies and clubs cater to a wide range of interests, promoting diversity and inclusivity. Social and cultural events are frequent, allowing students to engage with one another beyond the classroom. However, the university could do more to support extracurricular activities and promote a healthy work-life balance.

Studying at the University of Peshawar had both positives and serious drawbacks. On the positive side, the professors were knowledgeable and supportive, and the university has a strong reputation in the region. However, the Computer Science department was far from what one expects in today’s tech-driven world. The department building was old and poorly maintained, and it really needs to be shifted to a modern facility. Almost all processes were manual, even though this was supposed to be a Computer Science programme. The biggest issue was the lack of practical focus. Classes were mostly theory and exams, with little to no implementation of code or hands-on projects. Learning felt like memorization rather than gaining real technical skills. In addition, our education was regularly disturbed because almost every month there were strikes by employees or teachers due to salary issues. These strikes wasted valuable study time and created uncertainty. The university environment was also heavily influenced by politics, with groups, protests, and student clashes being common. All of this distracted from academics and made campus life stressful at times. Opinion about the University The University of Peshawar has potential, but its outdated system, constant strikes, and political environment take away from the learning experience. I appreciated the professors who genuinely wanted students to learn, but I disliked the infrastructure, lack of practical exposure, and continuous disruptions. Recommendation I would not recommend this university to students who want modern, practical, and industry-focused Computer Science education. It can give you a strong theoretical foundation, but you will need to put in extra effort outside the university to gain real skills and industry experience.

I had the opportunity to attend the University of Peshawar for my undergraduate and associate degree and overall, it was a positive experience. The campus is beautiful and well-maintained, with modern facilities and resources available to students. One thing that stood out to me was the diverse student body at the University of Peshawar. There were students from all over Pakistan and beyond, which made for a rich and enriching educational environment. One area that could be improved is the availability of extracurricular activities. While there were a few clubs and organizations to get involved in, I would have liked to see more options for students to get involved outside of their coursework. Overall, I would recommend the University of Peshawar to anyone considering pursuing higher education in Pakistan. The quality of education is high, and the campus is a wonderful place to learn and grow.

The University of Peshawar, commonly referred to as UoP, holds a special place as one of the oldest and most prestigious educational institutions in Pakistan. As a student, my experience there was a mix of academic rigor, cultural richness, and personal growth. Here’s a detailed account of my journey: Campus and Facilities The campus is sprawling and picturesque, surrounded by the beautiful landscape of Peshawar. It provides a calm and inspiring environment for learning. The iconic buildings, such as the Central Library and the lush green lawns, add to the charm. While the campus is well-maintained, some facilities, such as IT labs and lecture halls, could use modernization. The library is a treasure trove for research students, but digital resources are somewhat limited. Academic Experience The University offers a diverse range of programs across disciplines. The faculty is a blend of experienced professors and passionate lecturers, many of whom are highly qualified with PhDs from international institutions. While the academic environment is competitive, I felt there could be more emphasis on practical knowledge and industry linkages. Research opportunities are abundant, but students need to be proactive to make the most of them. Student Life Student life at UoP is vibrant. There’s always something happening, be it debates, cultural events, or sports tournaments. The university fosters diversity, as students from various provinces and even neighboring countries come to study here. This diversity adds a unique flavor to the campus culture. However, I did feel that more extracurricular opportunities, like internships or skill-based workshops, could enhance the overall student experience. Hostels and Accommodation The hostel life is memorable but challenging. The rooms are affordable and sufficient for basic needs, but the infrastructure needs improvement. The canteen food is decent, though not exceptional. A sense of community and lifelong friendships make up for the shortcomings. Challenges While UoP has a stellar reputation, it does face challenges: Administrative Efficiency: The bureaucracy can be slow, especially during exam registrations or result declarations. Infrastructure: Some labs, lecture halls, and equipment need upgrades to align with modern standards. Career Services: Career counseling and job placement services are limited, leaving students to rely on personal networks. Overall Experience Studying at the University of Peshawar is an enriching experience. It provides a solid foundation for both academic and personal growth. Despite its challenges, the university equips its students with the skills and resilience to succeed in life. For anyone seeking an affordable, diverse, and academically enriching institution, UoP is a great choice.

The University of Peshawar is one of the oldest and most respected universities in Pakistan. university: University of Engineering and Technology - NED
text: Having completed my Bachelor’s in Computer Science from NED University back in 2008, I can reflect upon my experiences with appreciation and a touch of constructive feedback. NED University was, without a doubt, a crucial stepping stone in my educational journey. The university was undeniably helpful and engaging, providing a strong foundation in the field of computer science. However, it wasn’t without its limitations. While we were taught all the essential principles and theories of computer science, there was a noticeable gap in practical applications. In retrospect, I believe that emphasizing practical skills should have been a top priority. On the plus side, NED University did offer reasonably good facilities. The canteen and common rooms were well-maintained spaces where students could unwind and socialize. However, it’s worth noting that there was room for improvement when it came to sports facilities, particularly in providing more opportunities for both male and female students to participate in sports activities. One aspect where NED University fell short was in the availability of extracurricular activities. Limited resources posed a significant challenge in this regard. Expanding extracurricular offerings would not only have enriched our overall university experience but also encouraged a more holistic development of students. Despite these observations, my overall impression of NED University remains positive. The professors were cooperative, always willing to support and guide us in our academic pursuits. Their dedication to our education was evident and played a crucial role in our learning journey. In conclusion, NED University provided a solid educational foundation in computer science. While there were areas for improvement, the university’s commitment to nurturing future professionals in the field was commendable. It’s an institution that leaves a lasting impression, and I am grateful for the knowledge and skills I acquired during my time there.

text: When it comes to engineering and technology education in Pakistan, the NED University of Engineering and Technology in Karachi stands as an institution par excellence. This prestigious university has earned its reputation through a combination of outstanding education, an inspiring environment, and a dedicated faculty. Here’s a closer look at what makes NED University a true gem: Education at Its Finest: NED University is renowned for its rigorous academic programs that prepare students for the challenges of the real world. The curriculum is designed to foster critical thinking, problem-solving skills, and hands-on experience, ensuring that graduates are well-equipped to excel in their careers. The faculty members are not just teachers but mentors who guide students on their academic journey. The university offers a wide range of undergraduate and postgraduate programs in various engineering and technology disciplines. The emphasis on research and innovation is evident, as students are encouraged to engage in cutting-edge projects that have real-world applications. This practical approach to learning is invaluable, as it bridges the gap between theory and practice, setting NED University apart from the rest. Inspiring and Inclusive Environment: NED University’s campus is a testament to the institution’s commitment to providing an exceptional environment for learning. The sprawling campus boasts state-of-the-art facilities, modern classrooms, and well-equipped laboratories that facilitate hands-on learning. The library is a treasure trove of knowledge, offering an extensive collection of books and resources to support students’ academic pursuits. Furthermore, NED University is a melting pot of diverse cultures and backgrounds. The inclusive and welcoming atmosphere encourages collaboration and the exchange of ideas among students from all walks of life. The campus is not just a place to study but a vibrant community where lifelong friendships are forged. Dedicated and Inspiring Faculty: The heart and soul of any educational institution are its teachers, and NED University takes pride in its exceptional faculty. The professors here are not just experts in their respective fields; they are passionate educators who go the extra mile to ensure their students’ success. Their dedication to teaching and research sets a high standard for academic excellence. The faculty members are approachable, readily available for guidance and support, and are known for their mentorship beyond the classroom. They inspire students to aim high, nurturing their talents and guiding them toward their career aspirations. In conclusion, NED University of Engineering and Technology, Karachi, is a shining beacon of educational excellence. Its commitment to providing top-notch education, fostering an inspiring environment, and nurturing exceptional teachers is what sets it apart. For anyone seeking a transformative educational experience in the field of engineering and technology, NED University is undoubtedly the place to be.

university: Mirpur University of Science and Technology - MUST
text: A Bachelor of Science (BS) in Computer Science is an undergraduate program that typically takes four years to complete. It focuses on the theory, design, and programming of computers and computer systems. Students learn programming languages, algorithms, data structures, software engineering, computer architecture, computer networks, artificial intelligence, and other relevant topics. The curriculum of a BS in Computer Science degree is designed to equip students with the skills and knowledge required to analyze and solve complex computing problems. Students learn to write efficient code, design and implement software solutions, and work effectively in a team environment. Graduates of a BS in Computer Science degree are in high demand in various industries, including technology, healthcare, finance, and many others. They can work as software developers, system analysts, data analysts, network administrators, cybersecurity experts, and more. They can also pursue advanced degrees in computer science or related fields.

BNU has, in my opinion, the most aesthetically designed and well-built campus. With a 1:1 ratio cricket pitch, a football court, multi-story cafeteria building, and many academic blocks, the campus brings together all these elements into one unified institution.

I did my undergrad in Applied Psychology, the program itself was a holistic approach to psychology in general. With theory there were placements and opportunities to go to hospitals and asylums to build cases and provide our own opinion to what the diagnosis will be. The only thing I did not like was the fact that our department did not have a separate building otherwise the whole experience throughout my four years were very nice. The student life is extremely fun and there’s no peer pressure of any sort anywhere. Everybody is accepting of everybody’s identities. In my opinion international students can come and easily gel in with the students and the teachers. BNU helps you find jobs by having job fairs and continuously introducing their students to new opportunities through events and emails even long after they’ve graduated.

The university is overall satisfactory but there is an issue of relative grading experienced by almost all students which causes added stress and anxiety to students. Other aspects of the university are pretty good and I would recommend people to join the university. However, being from the Psychology department, there are some students’ concerns regarding grading and assistance which need to be addressed properly.

There’s great emphasis on academics with a lot of the programs brought here as distance learning versions of top foreign universities. Overall, pretty good but some courses seem a bit too introductory and bit more rigour could make for an even better academic experience. Extra curriculars on the other hand are awesome. I have no complaints because I regularly have the chance to go to concerts, MUNs, trips, quizzes and charity drives. The only way I think it could be stepped up is offering scholarship funded trips abroad to students who perform exceptionally well in certain academic or non academic areas.

I studied at BNU, and my experience there was a mix of academic growth, personal development, and opportunities for involvement outside the classroom. The faculty was knowledgeable and supportive, especially in my major. I also appreciated the variety of student-led events, societies, and opportunities to take initiative. The campus had a vibrant environment that encouraged collaboration and learning. However, administrative processes (such as course registration or communication regarding policies) could be more streamlined and student-focused. Additionally, career counseling and job placement services could benefit from being more active and personalized. Overall, my time at BNU helped shape both my academic and interpersonal skills, and I’m grateful for the experience.

It was the best experience of my life. I learnt a lot from my teachers and fellows. I believed that they did their best to make us stand out in the crowd. Their mode of teaching was very simple but extremely efficient.

The almost six year degree is actually worth it, I’ve learnt marketing, business, professionalism, urban planning, architectural history, landscaping, sustainability and financial management through this. Student life gradually got better over the years; there were several international level events and a few national level ones that allowed us to meet different people; learn different cultures; visit history; learn languages and sports.

Ghulam Ishaq Khan Institute of Engineering Sciences and Technology (GIKI) has been a pivotal chapter in my educational journey. At GIKI, the pursuit of academic excellence is not merely a goal but a way of life. The institution’s commitment to delivering a world-class education is evident in its rigorous programs and distinguished faculty. The academic environment encourages critical thinking, problem-solving, and a deep understanding of engineering and technology. The faculty members are not only experts in their respective fields but also passionate educators, always ready to guide and inspire students in their quest for knowledge.

It is a good university, has dedicated teachers. You will learn a lot being in this university from studies to time management, to how to present yourself and live in a remote place. The courses are a little outdated but they teach the basics which helps a lot.

GIKI offers one of the challenging environment to its students. Faculty and staff are highly cooperative. It’s a fully residential institute. The exams are quite tough, to get good grades student has to put extra effort. GIKI offers merit and financial aid scholarships. In my opinion, paying for GIKI tuition fee is not worth it, if student has plans to stay in Pakistan. But again, GIKI graduates gets better chances of employment in multinational companies within Pakistan. There are many active societies. Life at GIKI is wholesome.

Apart from having topnotch education, GIK Institute is all about its campus life. Located in a small rural town,Topi, KPK (near Tarbela dam), GIK Institute attracts students from all over Pakistan who live on campus: in hostels. Administration is fairly cooperative and studies are pretty tough considering that the grading is relative and not absolute. GIK offers engineering related and management sciences related programmes. Through 25+ societies that are active throughout the year, it provides all the opportunities one can need in order to pursue his/her passion (engineering or arts related) while studying for the opted degree. What we all find difficult at first is adjusting to its environment and keeping a balance between studies and extra curricular activities. GIK Institute is also endorsed with the ‘Washington Accord’ (search it up if you don’t know what it is) which makes its degree more valuable to companies for employment purposes and international educational bodies for higher education. With nation’s one of the best laboratories, buildings and strongest alumni, this place tests you, polishes you and prepares you to thrive in every aspect: your future is safe if you somehow manage to graduate from here. P.s. This might not matter to most of you but to those it does; this place is very scenic with breath taking sunsets/ dawns all year around.

My experience at Air University Islamabad was both academically and personally rewarding. The university offers a strong curriculum, especially in AI, Data Science, and related fields. Several professors stood out for their engaging teaching methods and their ability to connect theoretical concepts with real-world applications. What truly shaped my journey, however, was my involvement in extracurricular activities. I served as the President of multiple student societies, where I led tech and cultural events, collaborated with external organizations, and mentored junior students. These roles gave me a platform to grow as a leader, problem-solver, and team player. I was honored to receive a Recognition Letter from the Vice Chancellor, which acknowledged my contributions to university life and leadership. That moment was a defining highlight of my time here. While the university has a disciplined and secure environment, there’s still room for improvement in infrastructure and student support services. But overall, for students who are motivated and willing to take initiative, Air University provides a great space to learn, lead, and grow.

Emphasizing case-based methodology, the department ensures that students are equipped to analyze real-world business scenarios effectively. With a highly competent and foreign-qualified faculty, students benefit from a rich blend of academic knowledge and industry insight, preparing them to meet global business challenges with confidence.

Air University in Islamabad, Pakistan, is a young but ambitious public university focused on research and academics. Here’s a breakdown of its pros and cons to help you decide if it’s the right fit for you: Likes: Strong Academics: Air University boasts a good reputation for academics, ranking 15th nationally in 2022 and achieving international recognition. They are particularly well-regarded for their engineering programs. Focus on Character Development: The university emphasizes not just academic achievement but also ethical and social values, fostering well-rounded graduates. Modern Campuses: With its main campus in Islamabad and additional campuses focused on aerospace and aviation, Air University offers modern facilities for a good learning environment. Dislikes: Limited History: Established in 2002, Air University is a relatively young institution compared to some established universities in Pakistan. This can mean a less developed alumni network and traditions. Potentially Competitive: Air University’s reputation attracts a competitive applicant pool, so admission may be challenging depending on the program. Limited Reviews: Finding student reviews of Air University can be difficult, making it harder to get a well-rounded picture of the student experience. Overall: Air University is a promising university with a focus on academic excellence and character development. Its modern facilities and strong faculty make it an attractive option for Pakistani students, particularly those interested in engineering or aerospace fields. However, its young age and competitive nature might be a consideration for some. Recommendation: If you’re a high-achieving student seeking a well-regarded academic program in Pakistan, Air University is definitely worth considering.  However, researching other universities and talking to current students (if possible) would be helpful for a more complete picture.

My time at Air University has been an incredible journey of growth and learning. When I graduate in 2022, it will be the first university to offer a degree in cyber security. It provides a demanding environment in which students must compete with one another to join Pakistan’s first cyber security batch of students.

Im a final year student at DHA Suffa currently pursuing my bachelors in Software Engineering and my overall experience has been positive. The thing that i personally like  the most are the frequent workshops, career fairs, events etc that gives industry exposure and networking opportunities to the students which can help them immensely in kickstarting their careers. Another thing that i like is the university’s International Education Resource Center (IERC) which has been sending students abroad for semester exchange programs, tours etc. They have been signing a lot of mou’s with universities around the globe. Mid tier universities in Karachi usually do not have this resource. Its a kind of a new thing for students here which attracts them and is quite exciting. One of the few dislikes that i had in the earlier days of my bachelors was the inefficiency of the management which has now been improved a lot and is still improving. I would recommend this institute to students interested in pursuing a tech related field which can include CS, CYS, DS, SE etc. Because you can get good industrial exposure and opportunities to go abroad. university: Dawood University of Engineering And Technology
text: As an architecture student at university, the experience is unique and distinct due to the nature of the field. Here are some key aspects as an architecture student I experience at university: 1. Design Studios: Architecture programs typically involve design studios, where students work on various projects under the guidance of faculty members. These studios provide a hands-on learning environment where students develop their design skills, create architectural drawings, build models, and present their work. 2. Architectural History and Theory: Students study the history of architecture and learn about different architectural styles, movements, and influential architects. We explore architectural theory and its application to design concepts. 3. Technical Skills Development: Architecture students learn technical skills essential to the profession, such as drafting, 3D modeling, rendering, computer-aided design (CAD), Building Information Modeling (BIM), and construction documentation. 4. Collaborative Projects: Architecture often involves collaborative work. Students frequently work in teams to complete projects, simulate real-world scenarios, and gain experience in teamwork, communication, and coordination. 5. Critiques and Reviews: Architecture education involves frequent critiques and reviews of student work. These sessions provide valuable feedback from faculty members and peers, allowing students to refine their designs and learn from constructive criticism. 6. Field Trips and Site Visits: Architecture programs often include field trips and site visits to architectural landmarks, construction sites, and urban environments. These experiences offer practical insights into real-world architecture, urban planning, and design principles. 7. Professional Networking: Architecture students have opportunities to interact with professionals in the field through guest lectures, industry events, and internships. These connections can be valuable for future career prospects. 8.Portfolio Development: Throughout their studies, architecture students compile a portfolio showcasing their best work and design capabilities. A strong portfolio is essential when seeking internships, jobs, or applying for graduate programs. 9. Time Commitment: Architecture programs are known for their rigorous workload and demanding schedules. Students often spend long hours in studio, working on projects, meeting deadlines, and balancing coursework. 10. Graduation Project: Towards the end of our studies, we typically undertake a comprehensive graduation project. This project allows us to demonstrate our skills, creativity, and understanding of architectural concepts. It’s important to note that the architecture student experience can vary between universities and programs. Different universities may have unique curricula, teaching methodologies, and resources. Additionally, individual experiences and personal commitment play a significant role in shaping the overall experience as an architecture student.
